rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Thao Thi Lan Nguyen'S Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Thao Thi Lan Nguyen's Motion to Strike or Tax Son's Memorandum of Costs is granted in part and denied in part. Son is the prevailing party and is entitled to costs as a matter of right. (C.C.P. Section 1032(a)(4).) Thao's request to strike Son's Memorandum of Costs is therefore denied. The request to tax costs is granted as to the $40 Request for Special Notice filing fee (item 1a) Son incurred before filing the Section 850 Petition. Son concedes in his opposition that this cost was premature and unnecessary to the litigation. The remainder of Thao's motion to tax is denied. Thao has failed to comply with CRC, Rule 3.1700(b)(2) and, as a result, both Son and the Court lack sufficient information as to which costs Thao finds objectionable. Further, the general categories of costs Thao refers to on page 3 of her opposition are allowable under C.C.P. Section 1033.5 and Thao did not meet her burden of demonstrating that the costs were unnecessary or unreasonable. (Foothill-DeAnza Community College Dist. v. Emerich (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 11, 29 (if the cost items set forth in the memorandum of costs appear to be proper charges under C.C.P. Section 1033.5, the burden is on the objecting party to show the costs were unnecessary or unreasonable.) Thao provides no legal support for her request to offset costs, and the Court declines to offset Son's costs by the amount sought in Thao's Memorandum of Costs. The prevailing party must submit a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 6/22/2016, at 4:00 p.m.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Son Nguyens Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Alternatively To Tax Costs Of Respondent Thao Thi Lan Nguyen
rulings.Rulings: Son Nguyen's Motion to Strike Thao Ngueyen's Memorandum of Costs is GRANTED. Son is the prevailing party and is entitled to costs as a matter of right. (C.C.P. Section 1032(a)(4).) The Court's Statement of Decision ordered Thao to "pay one-half of the amount she received upon the sale of the gold bars ($9,000) to the six children, i.e., $1,500 each." (SOD, p. 6:7-9.) This constitutes a net monetary recovery per the terms of Section 1032 and makes Son the "prevailing party" entitled to costs as a matter of right. The fact that Son did not prevail on all or most of his eleven causes of action does not change this analysis. Because Thao has not shown any basis for her own entitlement to costs, Son's motion to strike Thao's Memorandum of Costs in its entirety is granted. Son's alternative request to tax costs is moot in light of the Court's ruling. The prevailing party must submit a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than June 22, 2016, at 4:00 p.m.
